title: Split coded long-format choice data into balanced cross-validation folds
summary: Split coded long-format choice data into balanced cross-validation folds at the respondent level. Each fold gets a train.csv and test.csv, plus a fold_assignments.csv mapping respondents to folds.
description: Partitions a coded long-format choice CSV into `folds` cross-validation folds by assigning whole respondents (not individual rows) to folds. Splitting by respondent — and within respondent by whole choice sets — preserves the panel structure that hierarchical and latent-class choice models depend on. The output is laid out as one subdirectory per fold containing matching `train.csv` and `test.csv` files, suitable for direct consumption by `model-mnl-lg::cv-fit`, `model-mnl-lc::cv-fit`, and `model-mnl-hb-mh::cv-fit`.
usage:
[["choice-cv::split"]]
input = "<coded_long.csv>"
output_dir = "<dir>"
# optional:
folds = <int>
respondent_col = <int>
task_col = <int>
random_seed = <int>
balance_iterations = <int>
balance_early_stop = <int>

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the coded long-format CSV to split. The file must contain a respondent column and a task column (configurable via `respondent_col` / `task_col`); all other columns are carried through unchanged into the train/test outputs.

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. Created if it does not exist. The plugin writes one fold subdirectory per fold (each containing a train.csv and test.csv) plus a top-level fold_assignments.csv. Existing files in this directory may be overwritten — point at a fresh directory per run.

arguments.name: folds
arguments.description: Number of cross-validation folds to produce. Integer >= 2. Common choices are 5 (default) and 10. Higher fold counts shrink the test set per fold but increase the number of model fits required downstream.

arguments.name: respondent_col
arguments.description: 1-based column index of the respondent ID column. Default 1 (first column).

arguments.name: task_col
arguments.description: 1-based column index of the task (choice set) number column. Default 2 (second column).

arguments.name: random_seed
arguments.description: Seed for the underlying random number generator. 0 (default) uses an internal seed (123) for deterministic output. Provide a different non-zero value for an alternative reproducible assignment.

arguments.name: balance_iterations
arguments.description: Maximum iterations of the post-assignment balance loop. After the initial random assignment, the splitter performs swap proposals to even out fold sizes (measured in rows, not respondents). 1000 is comfortable for studies with up to ~5000 respondents.

arguments.name: balance_early_stop
arguments.description: Early-stop patience: stop the balance loop if no improvement is found for this many consecutive iterations. Integer >= 1.

arguments.name: fold_prefix
arguments.description: Prefix for fold subdirectory names. Default 'fold' produces fold_01, fold_02, etc.

arguments.name: train_filename
arguments.description: Filename for the training CSV inside each fold directory. Default 'train.csv'.

arguments.name: test_filename
arguments.description: Filename for the test CSV inside each fold directory. Default 'test.csv'.

arguments.name: assignment_filename
arguments.description: Filename for the fold-assignment summary CSV written at the top level of output_dir. Default 'fold_assignments.csv'.

details:
The split is two-phase. First, every distinct respondent ID is randomly assigned to one of `folds` test buckets using a seeded RNG. Second, an iterative balance loop runs up to `balance_iterations` swap proposals, each attempting to move a respondent between folds in order to reduce variance in fold size (measured in total rows, not respondent count). The loop exits early if no improvement is found for `balance_early_stop` consecutive iterations.

The splitter is deliberately respondent-level: for any individual respondent, every row appears in exactly one fold's test.csv and in `(folds - 1)` of the train.csv files. This is the only split policy that gives valid out-of-sample evaluations for hierarchical Bayes and latent class models.
value:
On success, `output_dir` contains:

- `{fold_prefix}_NN/train.csv` and `{fold_prefix}_NN/test.csv` for each fold.
- `fold_assignments.csv` — one row per respondent, recording which test fold they were assigned to.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, one `event.artifact.created` per file written, and a final `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: respondent_col and task_col are 1-based column indices, not column names.
The balance loop minimises row-count variance, not respondent-count variance.

see_also.description: Cross-validated multinomial logit fit consuming this split.
Cross-validated latent class fit consuming this split.
Cross-validated hierarchical Bayes MH fit consuming this split.

fields.name: input
fields.description: Path to the coded-long-format CSV file to split.

fields.name: output_dir
fields.description: Output directory. Each fold gets a subdirectory.

fields.name: folds
fields.description: Number of CV folds. Integer >= 2. Default: 5.

fields.name: respondent_col
fields.description: 1-based column index of the respondent ID. Default: 1.

fields.name: task_col
fields.description: 1-based column index of the task number. Default: 2.

fields.name: balance_iterations
fields.description: Max iterations for fold-size balancing. Default: 1000.

fields.name: balance_early_stop
fields.description: Stop early if no improvement for N iterations. Default: 20.

fields.name: fold_prefix
fields.description: Prefix for fold subdirectory names. Default: 'fold'.

fields.name: train_filename
fields.description: Training CSV filename inside each fold directory. Default: 'train.csv'.

fields.name: test_filename
fields.description: Test CSV filename inside each fold directory. Default: 'test.csv'.
